subject_profile.summary: Highly educated American, board-certified laboratory animal veterinarian (ACLAM), considering relocation.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Scores predate full v2 source-discipline re-run.
Gang-violence country-average overstates personal risk for residents outside affected suburbs.
Russia conflict-exposure is a genuine tail risk not fully captured by expected-value scoring.
Sept 2026 election could shift political/social scores at the margin.

economic.notes: Among the strongest economic profiles assessed: very low debt, disciplined fiscal framework, independent Riksbank, low-oil-exposure energy mix.

fiscal_state.notes: Deficit ~1.3% GDP; debt ~30% GDP (among lowest in developed world); post-1990s fiscal framework; rising but absorbable defense spend.

monetary_independence.notes: Riksbank strongly independent; rate held at 1.75%; credible framework.

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: SEK not a reserve currency; neutral.

inflation.notes: Below 2% target (~1.5% 2026, helped by food VAT cuts); krona appreciation; nuclear/hydro reduces oil exposure.

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalized majors; past Baltic-AML issues addressed; 2022-23 housing correction without systemic crisis.

trade_policy.notes: EU-delegated; strong export economy; indirect exposure to supply-chain disruption and US tariffs.

institutional.notes: Highest single-category score and highest confidence in the set. Genuinely excellent institutional baseline.

rule_of_law.notes: Top-5 globally (WJP); strong judicial independence; recent gang-related police/surveillance expansions via normal democratic process.

statistical_integrity.notes: SCB highly regarded; no interference; near top of indices.

civil_service_capacity.notes: Professional, independent; constitutionally protected agency independence (myndighetsoberoende).

press_freedom.notes: Top-5 RWB; world's oldest FOI act (1766); robust public and private media.

political_social.notes: Striking: excellent institutions but gang violence and immigration restrictiveness pull social state near neutral. Long-term composite improves (~+1) as violence trend declines.

political_stability.notes: Kristersson government stable since 2022; Sept 2026 election upcoming; SD rise within democratic norms.

civil_liberties.notes: Strong baseline; surveillance trajectory concerning (anonymous witnesses, expanded wiretapping, proposed military aid to police).

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Shifted from most-open to among-most-restrictive in ~5y; refugee/family-reunification cut; citizenship tightened; skilled-professional path still functional.

social_violence.notes: 621 explosion offences in 2025 (vs 162 in 2018); 62k in criminal networks; but 2026 shootings down sharply (15 vs 39 early-year); geographically concentrated.

demographics.notes: Immigration-cut growth slowdown; below-replacement fertility; integration challenges; skilled-labor shortages.

geopolitical.notes: Strong alliance reliability (credibility provider post-NATO accession) offset by direct Russia conflict-exposure tail risk.

alliance_reliability.notes: NATO member since 2024; deep Nordic-Baltic cooperation; building EU credibility as US reliability falls; leads NATO forward forces in Finland.

conflict_involvement.notes: Direct Russia threat as Baltic-adjacent NATO member; hybrid ops, airspace violations; conscription reintroduced; Article 5 tail risk real but not base case.

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: EU sanctions regime; no domestic capital controls; stable financial integration.

physical_practical.notes: Excellent infrastructure and low disaster exposure; healthcare strong; crime score the one complicating factor but still low by US/Mexico standards.

climate.notes: Relative beneficiary: longer growing seasons, lower heating costs; some forest-fire/Baltic exposure.

healthcare.notes: Universal single-payer; strong outcomes; some primary-care/specialist wait times.

infrastructure.notes: Excellent transport, world-leading broadband, low-carbon stable grid (nuclear/hydro), strong transit.

crime_safety.notes: Homicide ~1/100k (low by US/Mexico standard, high by Nordic standard); gang violence concentrated; most of country very safe; trend improving.

disaster_insurance.notes: Low disaster exposure (minimal quake, no hurricanes); functional insurance; strong response capacity.

personal_fit.notes: Strong life-sciences sector (AstraZeneca HQ, Karolinska, Uppsala BMC, Lund, SLU, Gothenburg). ECLAM-recognized; ACLAM workable via IACLAM temporary diplomate status. Higher friction than Canada (authority recognition, possible Swedish-language requirement for clinical work; research often English). EU Blue Card available. Salaries lower absolute (SEK 700K-1.2M) but strong benefits.

labanimal_vet_fit.notes: Institutions accustomed to hiring internationally; AAALAC recognizes ACLAM as equivalent; +5 not +7 due to language and process friction.

summary: Strongest overall profile in the comparison. Highest institutional (+6.4) and among the highest economic (+3.7) scores. Takes hits on political/social (gang violence, immigration restrictiveness) and geopolitical (direct Russia exposure). Edges Canada slightly once personal fit is included, though the two are effectively tied. Highest-quality option but higher-friction relocation than Canada.
